sae_features.label: Extracellular mucin-like disordered stalks
sae_features.description: Extracellular/lumenal mucin-like low‑complexity stalks: long Ser/Thr/Pro‑rich (PTS) or analogous glyco‑acceptor segments that are intrinsically disordered and exposed to the secretory pathway, including plant AGP Hyp‑glycomodules and disordered lumenal “stem” regions of secretory enzymes; typically heavily O‑glycosylated (often with clustered N‑glycosites) and positioned between a signal peptide and a transmembrane helix or GPI anchor.

sae_features.label: TM-flanking low-complexity IDRs
sae_features.description: Low‑complexity, Ser/Thr/Pro–rich intrinsically disordered segments that flank signal peptides or transmembrane helices in secretory‑pathway and cell‑surface proteins—i.e., mucin‑like lumenal ectodomains and glycan‑attachment tracts—and, by extension, analogous proline/polar/charged disordered cytosolic tails; the feature avoids transmembrane helices and well‑folded catalytic domains.

sae_features.label: Extracellular mucin-like low-complexity tracts
sae_features.description: Extracellular mucin-like/proteoglycan-style low‑complexity regions: long, intrinsically disordered Pro/Thr/Ser (±Gly)-rich “stalks/linkers” that carry dense O-glycans and often GAG attachment motifs in secreted and single‑pass membrane proteins, including non‑collagenous collagen linkers and extended ectodomains of adhesion/receptor proteins

sae_features.label: Extracellular mucin-like PTS segments
sae_features.description: Extracellular mucin-like PTS segments: long Ser/Thr/Pro-rich, low‑complexity, disordered stalk/linker regions prone to dense mucin‑type O‑glycosylation, typically located just after the signal peptide or immediately N‑terminal to a transmembrane helix or structured ectodomain in cell‑surface and viral glycoproteins.

sae_features.label: Type I cytosolic tails
sae_features.description: Cytosolic C-terminal tails of single-pass type I membrane (and viral envelope) proteins, especially the juxtamembrane, intrinsically disordered segments enriched in acidic/Ser/Thr/Pro residues and tyrosine-based endocytic/signaling motifs (NPxY/YENPTY), which mediate adaptor binding, phosphorylation, and trafficking.

sae_features.label: Juxtamembrane ectodomain stalk
sae_features.description: Extracellular juxtamembrane “stalk/ectodomain stem” immediately N‑terminal to a transmembrane helix in metazoan cell‑surface glycoproteins (and analogous luminal loops before TMs in multipass proteins). These segments are typically Pro/Ser/Thr/Gly- and acidic‑rich, often low‑complexity or flexible with GP/PG clusters but may include short amphipathic helices; they frequently harbor N-/O‑glycosylation and protease shedding/processing sites. When an EGF‑ or SEA‑like module abuts the membrane, the feature extends into the C‑terminal part of that module.

sae_features.label: Cytoplasmic juxtamembrane regulatory hub
sae_features.description: Membrane-proximal cytoplasmic juxtamembrane segment of single-pass cell-surface glycoproteins: a short, often amphipathic/basic region immediately C‑terminal to the transmembrane helix that associates with the inner leaflet and serves as a regulatory/adaptor-binding hub enriched for Lys/Arg and Ser/Thr (with frequent phosphorylation) and sometimes nearby palmitoylated cysteines.

sae_features.label: Type I single-pass transmembrane helix
sae_features.description: Single-pass type I membrane-protein transmembrane alpha-helix (stop-transfer/signal-anchor) that anchors large ectodomains of metazoan cell-surface receptors/adhesion molecules and analogous viral envelope glycoproteins
